title: System and Method for Zero-Knowledge Verifiable Reconstruction of Neural Networks from On-Chain Latent Seeds
author: Stephenson, MJ
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
On-Chain LLM Permanence and Zero-Knowledge Verified Reconstruction  
 This deposit contains the complete research artifact for the POKEBALL and MASTERBALL protocols, developed at the University of Waterloo. 
  POKEBALL  is the first demonstration of a reconstructable large language model weight representation stored permanently in a smart contract on a public blockchain. A hypernetwork is trained to compress the full tensor weight distribution of TinyLlama-1.1B (Q4_K_M quantization, 637 MB) into a 1,024-byte latent seed vector, achieving a 654,296× compression ratio. The seed is inscribed as immutable contract storage on the Aleph Zero blockchain (contract address: 5CXjoRc...q2Kn, deployed April 19, 2026) via an ink! 4.3 smart contract, queryable by any on-chain or off-chain agent. Given the open-source hypernetwork architecture and the on-chain seed, the full approximate weight distribution of TinyLlama-1.1B is deterministically reconstructable at any future time with no dependency on any centralized server. 
  MASTERBALL  extends POKEBALL with cryptographic execution guarantees via a Zero-Knowledge Proof of Honest Decompression (MB-PoHD). A custom STARK over the Goldilocks field (p = 2⁶⁴ − 2³² + 1) compresses a naïve 2³⁰-row execution trace to 2¹⁷ rows through structured MAC routing and log-derivative activation lookup arguments. The on-chain ink! verifier runs at approximately 170,400 gas on Aleph Zero — a 2¹³-fold improvement over generic RISC-V zkVM compilation. Liminal privacy integration shields the latent seed from public exposure while preserving verifiability. 
 A Canadian patent application has been filed with the Canadian Intellectual Property Office (CIPO #3309025) under the title  System and Method for Zero-Knowledge Verifiable Reconstruction of Neural Networks from On-Chain Latent Seeds  (filed April 20, 2026). 
  Contents of this deposit (Embargoed until 2026-12-31):  
 
 
  POKEBALL Protocol preprint (PDF):  The foundational research paper on verifiable neural network reconstruction. 
 
 
  MASTERBALL Protocol Specification (PDF):  Technical deep-dive into the proprietary ZK-arithmetization and  $2^{13}$  gas-efficiency circuits. 
 
 
  Canadian Patent Application (PDF):  Official CIPO filing confirmation for the system and method (Filed: 2026-04-20). 
 
 
  Hypernetwork Weights (hypernetwork_best.pt):  The core trained  state_dict  enabling 654,296x compression of model parameters. 
 
 
  Hypernetwork training code (train_tinyllama.py):  Python implementation for reproducing the latent seed mapping. 
 
 
  On-chain seed records (JSON):  Production seeds and schema examples for decentralized verification. 
 
 
  High-resolution figures (PNG):  All charts and architectural diagrams from the protocol documentation.

On-Chain LLM Permanence and Zero-Knowledge Verified Reconstruction

  POKEBALL  is the first demonstration of a reconstructable large language model weight representation stored permanently in a smart contract on a public blockchain. A hypernetwork is trained to compress the full tensor weight distribution of TinyLlama-1.1B (Q4_K_M quantization, 637 MB) into a 1,024-byte latent seed vector, achieving a 654,296× compression ratio. The seed is inscribed as immutable contract storage on the Aleph Zero blockchain (contract address: 5CXjoRc...q2Kn, deployed April 19, 2026) via an ink! 4.3 smart contract, queryable by any on-chain or off-chain agent. Given the open-source hypernetwork architecture and the on-chain seed, the full approximate weight distribution of TinyLlama-1.1B is deterministically reconstructable at any future time with no dependency on any centralized server.

A custom STARK over the Goldilocks field (p = 2⁶⁴ − 2³² + 1) compresses a naïve 2³⁰-row execution trace to 2¹⁷ rows through structured MAC routing and log-derivative activation lookup arguments. The on-chain ink! verifier runs at approximately 170,400 gas on Aleph Zero — a 2¹³-fold improvement over generic RISC-V zkVM compilation. Liminal privacy integration shields the latent seed from public exposure while preserving verifiability. 
 A Canadian patent application has been filed with the Canadian Intellectual Property Office (CIPO #3309025) under the title  System and Method for Zero-Knowledge Verifiable Reconstruction of Neural Networks from On-Chain Latent Seeds  (filed April 20, 2026).
